ucs.title: [LLM] Sea Turtle (Marbled Dust) DNS hijack — anomalous resolution flip on corporate auth/SSO portals
ucs.description: Sea Turtle's signature TTP is compromising upstream DNS providers / ccTLD registrars to briefly repoint NS or A records for a victim's webmail, VPN, ADFS, or SSO portal to actor-controlled infrastructure for credential harvesting (typically with valid Let's Encrypt certs). This hunts the downstream symptom — the same internal hostnames suddenly resolving to public IPs that have never been seen as their answer set in the 30-day org baseline.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count, min(_time) as first_seen, dc(host) as host_count, values(host) as hosts from datamodel=Network_Resolution.DNS where DNS.message_type=Response (DNS.query IN ("*webmail*","*owa*","*autodiscover*","*vpn*","*sso*","*adfs*","*sts*","*portal*","*remote*","*exchange*","*citrix*")) earliest=-1h by DNS.query DNS.answer | rename "DNS.*" as * | where NOT cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8", answer) AND NOT cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12", answer) AND NOT cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16", answer) AND NOT cidrmatch("127.0.0.0/8", answer) | search NOT [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Resolution.DNS where DNS.message_type=Response earliest=-30d latest=-1h by DNS.query DNS.answer | rename "DNS.*" as * | fields query answer] | iplocation answer | table first_seen query answer Country City host_count hosts | sort - first_seen
ucs.kql:
// Sea Turtle DNS hijack — flip in resolved IP for sensitive auth/VPN portals
let lookback = 30d;
let recent   = 1h;
let critical_substrings = dynamic([
    "webmail","owa","autodiscover","vpn","sso","adfs","sts",
    "portal","remote","exchange","citrix","mail.","smtp"
]);
let baseline =
    DeviceEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(lookback) .. ago(recent))
    | where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
    | extend AF = parse_json(AdditionalFields)
    | extend Q  = tolower(tostring(AF.QueryName)),
             IPs = tostring(AF.IPAddresses)
    | where isnotempty(Q) and isnotempty(IPs)
    | mv-expand IP = parse_json(IPs)
    | extend IP = tostring(IP)
    | summarize by Q, IP;
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(recent)
| where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
| extend AF = parse_json(AdditionalFields)
| extend Q  = tolower(tostring(AF.QueryName)),
         IPs = tostring(AF.IPAddresses)
| where isnotempty(Q) and isnotempty(IPs)
| where Q has_any (critical_substrings)
| mv-expand IP = parse_json(IPs)
| extend IP = tostring(IP)
| where not(ipv4_is_private(IP)) and not(IP startswith "127.") and not(IP startswith "169.254.")
| join kind=leftanti baseline on Q, IP                       // never seen this answer before
| summarize FirstSeen   = min(Timestamp),
            HostCount   = dcount(DeviceName),
            SampleHosts = make_set(DeviceName, 10),
            Resolvers   = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 5)
            by Q, IP
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sea Turtle DNS hijack — ASIM cross-vendor variant (Windows DNS, Umbrella, Infoblox, Linux resolvers...)
let lookback = 30d;
let recent   = 1h;
let critical_substrings = dynamic([
    "webmail","owa","autodiscover","vpn","sso","adfs","sts",
    "portal","remote","exchange","citrix","mail.","smtp"
]);
let baseline =
    ImDnsActivity
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(lookback) .. ago(recent))
    | where isnotempty(DnsResponseName) and isnotempty(DnsQuery)
    | extend Q = tolower(DnsQuery), Resp = tostring(DnsResponseName)
    | summarize by Q, Resp;
ImDnsActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(recent)
| where isnotempty(DnsResponseName)
| extend Q = tolower(DnsQuery), Resp = tostring(DnsResponseName)
| where Q has_any (critical_substrings)
| where Resp matches regex @"^\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+$"               // strip CNAMEs, keep A answers
| where not(ipv4_is_private(Resp)) and not(Resp startswith "127.") and not(Resp startswith "169.254.")
| join kind=leftanti baseline on Q, Resp
| summarize FirstSeen   = min(TimeGenerated),
            HostCount   = dcount(SrcHostname),
            SampleHosts = make_set(SrcHostname, 10)
            by Q, Resp
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Generic DNS-hijack detections look at TXT volume or NXDOMAIN; Sea Turtle's actual playbook is to swap A records of high-value auth FQDNs (webmail/VPN/ADFS) for a few hours. Anchoring on (a) the specific FQDN keyword set Sea Turtle has historically targeted and (b) anti-baselining the answer IP per (Q, IP) pair surfaces exactly the brief resolution flip that drives credential capture, while ignorin

ucs.title: [LLM] Sea Turtle (Teal Kurma / Marbled Dust) SnappyTCP nohup-detached reverse shell from compromised Linux web server
ucs.description: After web-shell drop on a Linux web server (Apache/nginx/php-fpm) Sea Turtle launches its 'SnappyTCP' implant via `nohup ./bin &` so the reverse shell survives the parent web request — Talos explicitly tied this nohup detachment pattern (T1564.011) to G1041. This catches the web-user → nohup → shell-with-TCP-redirector chain in a single process_creation event.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count, min(_time) as first_seen, values(Processes.process) as cmdlines, values(Processes.process_path) as paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="nohup" OR Processes.process_name="nohup" OR Processes.process="nohup *" OR Processes.parent_process="nohup *") AND (Processes.user IN ("www-data","apache","nginx","httpd","tomcat","apache2","daemon") OR Processes.parent_process_name IN ("httpd","apache2","nginx","php-fpm","php-cgi","php")) earliest=-7d by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process, "(?i)/dev/tcp/|/dev/udp/|-e\s+/bin/|bash\s+-i|sh\s+-i|0>&1|exec\s+\d+<>|nc(at)?\s+.*-e|socat\s+.*exec") OR match(process_path, "(?i)/tmp/|/dev/shm/|/var/tmp/") | sort - first_seen
ucs.kql:
// Sea Turtle SnappyTCP — nohup + web-server user + reverse-shell tradecraft
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName in~ ("www-data","apache","nginx","httpd","tomcat","apache2","daemon")
     or AccountName            in~ ("www-data","apache","nginx","httpd","tomcat","apache2","daemon")
     or InitiatingProcessFileName       in~ ("apache2","httpd","nginx","php-fpm","php-cgi","php")
     or InitiatingProcessParentFileName in~ ("apache2","httpd","nginx","php-fpm","php-cgi","php")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "nohup"
     or ProcessCommandLine          startswith "nohup "
     or InitiatingProcessCommandLine startswith "nohup "
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
        "/dev/tcp/","/dev/udp/","-e /bin/sh","-e /bin/bash",
        "bash -i","sh -i","0>&1","exec 5<>","socat"," nc -e"," ncat -e")
     or FolderPath has_any ("/tmp/","/dev/shm/","/var/tmp/","/var/www/","/usr/share/nginx/")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          WebUser     = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          Grandparent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          Parent      = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child       = FileName,
          ChildPath   = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sea Turtle SnappyTCP — ASIM (works for MDE-Linux, AuditD, CrowdStrike, Sysmon-for-Linux)
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where DvcOs has "linux" or DvcOs has "Linux"
| where ActorUsername       in~ ("www-data","apache","nginx","httpd","tomcat","apache2","daemon")
     or ParentProcessName   in~ ("apache2","httpd","nginx","php-fpm","php-cgi","php","nohup")
| where ParentProcessName =~ "nohup"
     or TargetProcessCommandLine  startswith "nohup "
     or ParentProcessCommandLine  startswith "nohup "
| where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (
        "/dev/tcp/","/dev/udp/","-e /bin/sh","-e /bin/bash",
        "bash -i","sh -i","0>&1","exec 5<>","socat"," nc -e"," ncat -e")
     or TargetProcessName has_any ("/tmp/","/dev/shm/","/var/tmp/")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ParentProcessName, ParentProcessCommandLine,
          TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine, SHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Sea Turtle (Marbled Dust / Teal Kurma) SnappyTCP nohup-detached reverse shell
id: 7e2b3c8a-9f4d-4d11-9a25-5d3b2c7f01a4
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects Sea Turtle (G1041) post-web-shell tradecraft on Linux web servers — a nohup-detached
  shell or binary launched under a web-server account (www-data / apache / nginx / php-fpm)
  with reverse-shell command-line indicators. Talos and ENISA both attribute this nohup
  pattern (T1564.011) to the SnappyTCP implant.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1041/
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/sea-turtle-keeps-its-head-down/
  - https://www.ncsc.gov.tr/sea-turtle
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1059.004
  - attack.t1564.011
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.g1041
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection_web_user:
    User|contains:
      - 'www-data'
      - 'apache'
      - 'nginx'
      - 'httpd'
      - 'tomcat'
      - 'daemon'
  selection_web_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '/apache2'
      - '/httpd'
      - '/nginx'
      - '/php-fpm'
      - '/php-cgi'
      - '/php'
  selection_nohup:
    - ParentImage|endswith: '/nohup'
    - CommandLine|startswith: 'nohup '
    - ParentCommandLine|startswith: 'nohup '
  selection_revshell:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/dev/tcp/'
      - '/dev/udp/'
      - '-e /bin/sh'
      - '-e /bin/bash'
      - 'bash -i'
      - 'sh -i'
      - '0>&1'
      - 'exec 5<>'
      - 'socat '
      - ' nc -e'
      - ' ncat -e'
  condition: (selection_web_user or selection_web_parent) and selection_nohup and selection_revshell
falsepositives:
  - Sysadmin troubleshooting scripts run under the web user (rare)
  - Legitimate background workers detached with nohup that happen to use bash -i
level: high
ucs.rationale: Generic 'reverse shell from web server' rules over-fire on dev sandboxes; this rule pins three Sea-Turtle-specific facets simultaneously — (1) execution under a web-server identity (post-web-shell foothold, T1505.003), (2) the nohup detachment that Talos called out as their persistence trick (T1564.011) so the implant outlives the HTTP request, and (3) classic SnappyTCP-style TCP-redirector comman
